seoTitle: Thornton Beach vs Thornville — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Thornton Beach vs Thornville: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Thornton Beach and Thornville.
heading: Thornton Beach vs Thornville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Thornton Beach, Queensland and Thornville, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Thornton Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Thornville (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Thornton Beach or Thornville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Thornton Beach has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Thornville scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Thornton Beach scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Thornville scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Thornton Beach or Thornville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Thornton Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
